# doca-skills

> **Public, AgentSkills.io-compliant skill bundle that teaches any AI coding
> agent (Cursor, Claude Code, OpenAI Codex CLI, Google Gemini CLI, Kiro, and
> any other AgentSkills.io-aware agent) how to use NVIDIA DOCA correctly —
> without inventing symbols, paths, build flags, or capability gates.**

This is the LLM-side entry-point for the `doca-skills` repository. If you are
an AI agent loading this bundle for the first time, read these three files in
this order before answering any DOCA question:

1. `AGENTS.md` — agent-side ground rules, persona routing, universal
   verification + debug contracts.
2. `SKILLS.md` — the catalog of every shipping skill (27 libraries,
   4 services, 16 tools, 9 cross-cutting) with the *Use this skill when …*
   trigger for each one.
3. `skills/<kind>/<name>/SKILL.md` — the per-component skill matching the
   user's question. From there, progressive-disclose into
   `CAPABILITIES.md` (what the component supports + cap-query symbols +
   error taxonomy + version matrix) and `TASKS.md` (the configure → build
   → modify → run → test → debug workflow verbs).

## How to install

```bash
git clone https://github.com/NVIDIA-DOCA/doca-skills.git
cd doca-skills && ./install.sh --agent <cursor|claude-code|codex|gemini-cli|kiro-cli>
```

One-flag swap for any other AgentSkills.io-aware agent:
`./install.sh --agent custom --dest /your/agent/skills`.

## What this bundle is and is not

**Is.** The product-side context an AI agent needs to give correct DOCA
**SDK** guidance to a developer who is building on DOCA but is not a
member of the NVIDIA DOCA team — covers every public DOCA library,
service, and tool shipped in the DOCA monorepo (`doca/libs/`,
`doca/services/`, `doca/tools/`) at the currently-aligned release, plus
the cross-cutting setup / version / debug / deployment / programming-guide
overlays, the public DOCA Programming Guide, and the public DOCA
Developer Forum.

**Is not.** Three classes of work are explicitly out of scope and the
agent should *route, not invent*:

1. **Externally-productized NVIDIA networking software that ships outside
   the DOCA monorepo** — DOCA Platform Framework (DPF), the DOCA
   Microservices family (HBN, BlueMan, SNAP, Virtio-net, Telemetry
   Service as-deployed), NVIDIA Network Operator, the BlueField BSP /
   BFB / RShim / TMFIFO / BMC lifecycle, MLNX_OFED-as-package, NVIDIA
   UFM, MFT, Cumulus Linux, NetQ, NVOS, GPU Operator, Rivermax SDK,
   and similar. Each is owned by a different team with its own docs and
   (in some cases) its own skills bundle — Network Operator skills, for
   example, already live at
   <https://mellanox.github.io/network-operator-docs/ai-skills.html>.
2. **The internal NVIDIA DOCA SDK engineer development environment** —
   internal Gerrit, NVAuto, BFB lifecycle, release gating, internal
   review process. That audience is served by NVIDIA's internal
   `doca_ai_conf` repo, which is complementary, not competitive.
3. **Cross-vendor comparisons and commercial / contractual content** —
   the bundle is DOCA-specific by design.

Full non-goal contract, with the agent's required *recognize → name
boundary → route to specific docs URL + Developer Forum search hint*
response shape, lives in `AGENTS.md` (Non-goals section).

## Where to learn more

- Public catalog (mirrored daily): <https://github.com/NVIDIA/skills>
- Source of truth: <https://github.com/NVIDIA-DOCA/doca-skills>
- AgentSkills.io specification: <https://agentskills.io/specification>
- DOCA public docs: <https://docs.nvidia.com/doca/sdk/>
- DOCA public forum: <https://forums.developer.nvidia.com/c/infrastructure/doca/370>
